heading: Air quality monitoring
label: 7619 Air quality monitoring
notes: Amendments2005— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, substituted “After notice and opportunity for public hearing” for “Not later than one year after , and after notice and opportunity for public hearing”, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 109–59August 7, 1977

amendments: Amendments2005— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, substituted “After notice and opportunity for public hearing” for “Not later than one year after , and after notice and opportunity for public hearing”, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 109–59August 7, 1977

Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise expressly provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 1977 Amendment note under .Aug. 7, 1977section 406(d) of Pub. L. 95–95section 7401 of this title

children.text: utilizes uniform air quality monitoring criteria and methodology and measures such air quality according to a uniform air quality index,

provides for air quality monitoring stations in major urban areas and other appropriate areas throughout the United States to provide monitoring such as will supplement (but not duplicate) air quality monitoring carried out by the States required under any applicable implementation plan,
provides for daily analysis and reporting of air quality based upon such uniform air quality index, and
provides for recordkeeping with respect to such monitoring data and for periodic analysis and reporting to the general public by the Administrator with respect to air quality based upon such data.

children.heading: Air quality monitoring data influenced by exceptional events
children.label: b Air quality monitoring data influenced by exceptional events
i
children.text: affects air quality;

children.label: ii
children.text: is not reasonably controllable or preventable;

children.label: iii
children.text: is an event caused by human activity that is unlikely to recur at a particular location or a natural event; and

children.label: iv
children.text: is determined by the Administrator through the process established in the regulations promulgated under paragraph (2) to be an exceptional event.

children.label: i
children.text: stagnation of air masses or meteorological inversions;

children.label: ii
children.text: a meteorological event involving high temperatures or lack of precipitation; or

children.label: iii
children.text: air pollution relating to source noncompliance.

children.heading: Proposed regulations
children.label: A Proposed regulations
children.text: Not later than , after consultation with Federal land managers and State air pollution control agencies, the Administrator shall publish in the Federal Register proposed regulations governing the review and handling of air quality monitoring data influenced by exceptional events.March 1, 2006

children.heading: Final regulations
children.label: B Final regulations
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date on which the Administrator publishes proposed regulations under subparagraph (A), and after providing an opportunity for interested persons to make oral presentations of views, data, and arguments regarding the proposed regulations, the Administrator shall promulgate final regulations governing the review and handling or air quality monitoring data influenced by an exceptional event that are consistent with paragraph (3).1So in original. Probably should be “of”.1

children.label: i
children.text: the principle that protection of public health is the highest priority;

children.label: ii
children.text: the principle that timely information should be provided to the public in any case in which the air quality is unhealthy;

children.label: iii
children.text: the principle that all ambient air quality data should be included in a timely manner, an appropriate Federal air quality database that is accessible to the public;2So in original. Probably should be followed by “in”.2

children.label: iv
children.text: the principle that each State must take necessary measures to safeguard public health regardless of the source of the air pollution; and

children.label: v
children.text: the principle that air quality data should be carefully screened to ensure that events not likely to recur are represented accurately in all monitoring data and analyses.

children.label: i
children.text: the occurrence of an exceptional event must be demonstrated by reliable, accurate data that is promptly produced and provided by Federal, State, or local government agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: a clear causal relationship must exist between the measured exceedances of a national ambient air quality standard and the exceptional event to demonstrate that the exceptional event caused a specific air pollution concentration at a particular air quality monitoring location;

children.label: iii
children.text: there is a public process for determining whether an event is exceptional; and

children.label: iv
children.text: there are criteria and procedures for the Governor of a State to petition the Administrator to exclude air quality monitoring data that is directly due to exceptional events from use in determinations by the Administrator with respect to exceedances or violations of the national ambient air quality standards.

children.label: A
children.text: Guidance on the identification and use of air quality data affected by exceptional events (July 1986).

children.label: B
children.text: Areas affected by PM–10 natural events, .May 30, 1996

children.label: C
children.text: Appendices I, K, and N to part 50 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations.
